title: rz3: a deterministic, exact-rational SMT solver in pure Rust
description: rz3 is a pure-Rust SMT (Satisfiability Modulo Theories) solver built on a DPLL(T)/CDCL architecture. It computes over arbitrary-precision rationals (no floating-point in the decision core), is deterministic run-to-run (verified by an n=30 bit-identical harness), and has zero native dependencies, so it deploys to WebAssembly. Its linear-arithmetic core is complete and unconditionally terminating: strict inequalities use a symbolic delta-infinitesimal (Dutertre-de Moura) and the feasibility Simplex avoids cycling via a lexicographic epsilon-perturbation. Dual-licensed under MIT OR Apache-2.0.
